reasoning: Chancroid is a bacterial infection caused by Haemophilus ducreyi. It is easily treated and cured with a single dose of antibiotics like azithromycin or ceftriaxone. Because it is highly curable and its incidence has been steadily declining globally, there is virtually no public health or economic incentive to fund the costly and lengthy process of developing a commercial vaccine. Currently, there are no chancroid vaccine candidates in late-stage clinical trials, and developing an approved vaccine typically takes many years or decades. The latest known value on Wikipedia’s "List of infectious diseases" is "No," and absent any advanced vaccine candidates, it is extremely improbable that a vaccine will be developed, approved, and added to the Wikipedia list by the resolution date.
